title: Missing API Versioning Strategy
description: API paths do not include a version segment, making it impossible to evolve the API without breaking consumers. Versioning is a core REST maturity requirement.
check_pattern: paths do not start with /v1/ or /v2/ or version prefix, no version in path
fix_guidance: Prefix all API paths with a version segment such as /v1/ or /v2/. Alternatively, use the info.version field and document the versioning strategy in the description.

title: Missing operationId
description: Operations lack unique operationId fields, making it difficult to generate client SDKs, reference operations in documentation, and maintain the API lifecycle.
check_pattern: operations missing operationId field, endpoints without unique operation identifiers
fix_guidance: Add a unique operationId to every operation using camelCase or snake_case convention, e.g., getUserById, createOrder, listProducts.

title: Improper HTTP Method Usage
description: Operations use incorrect HTTP methods for their intent. GET operations should not modify state. POST should create resources. PUT/PATCH should update. DELETE should remove.
check_pattern: GET endpoints with request bodies, POST endpoints for retrieval, missing DELETE for remove operations
fix_guidance: Align HTTP methods with their RFC semantics: GET for retrieval, POST for creation, PUT for full update, PATCH for partial update, DELETE for removal.

title: Inconsistent Naming Conventions
description: Path parameters, query parameters, and schema properties use inconsistent naming conventions mixing camelCase, snake_case, and kebab-case.
check_pattern: mixed naming conventions across paths and schemas, inconsistent parameter naming
fix_guidance: Adopt a single naming convention (snake_case recommended for JSON APIs) and apply it consistently across all paths, parameters, and schema properties.

title: Missing Pagination on Collection Endpoints
description: Collection endpoints (returning arrays or lists) lack pagination parameters, causing performance issues and exposing unlimited data retrieval.
check_pattern: GET endpoints returning arrays without page, limit, offset, or cursor parameters
fix_guidance: Add pagination query parameters (page, limit or offset, cursor) to all collection endpoints. Document the maximum page size. Return pagination metadata in responses.

title: Missing Tags on Operations
description: Operations are not grouped with tags, making API documentation hard to navigate and preventing proper SDK organization.
check_pattern: operations missing tags field, untagged endpoints
fix_guidance: Add meaningful tags to every operation to group related endpoints. Define tag descriptions in the top-level tags array. Use domain-driven naming like Users, Orders, Products.

title: Missing Reusable Components
description: Schemas, parameters, and responses are defined inline repeatedly instead of using the components section for reuse, leading to inconsistency and maintenance overhead.
check_pattern: inline schema definitions repeated across multiple endpoints, no components section defined
fix_guidance: Move repeated schemas to components/schemas. Create reusable parameters in components/parameters. Define standard responses in components/responses and reference with $ref.

title: Non-RESTful Resource Naming
description: Paths use verbs instead of nouns for resource naming, violating REST principles. Paths should represent resources not actions.
check_pattern: paths containing verbs like /getUser, /createOrder, /deleteItem instead of nouns
fix_guidance: Use plural nouns for resource paths: /users instead of /getUsers, /orders instead of /createOrder. Actions should be expressed through HTTP methods.
